# Varity

> Build, deploy, and monetize apps with one command. Auth, database, storage, and payments included.

## What is Varity

Varity is a developer platform that provides open-source packages and a CLI for building, deploying, and monetizing production applications. Install two packages, run one command, and your app is live with auth, storage, database, and hosting already configured.

- Target audience: Web developers (React, Next.js, TypeScript)
- No blockchain knowledge required â€” infrastructure complexity is fully abstracted
- 70% cheaper than AWS/GCP/Azure

## Packages

### @varity-labs/sdk (v2.0.0-beta.14)
Core SDK for database, storage, and app configuration.

```typescript
import { db } from '@varity-labs/sdk';

const users = db.collection<User>('users');
await users.add({ name: 'Alice', email: 'alice@example.com' });
const all = await users.get();
await users.update('user-id', { name: 'Bob' });
await users.delete('user-id');
```

Key exports: `db`, `Database`, `Collection`, `createApp`, `resolveCredentials`, `logCredentialUsage`, `VERSION`
Database: `.get()` returns all docs â€” filter client-side. No server-side queries or `.where()`.

### @varity-labs/ui-kit (v2.0.0-beta.15)
React components for auth, dashboards, and UI.

```typescript
import { PrivyStack, PrivyLoginButton, PrivyProtectedRoute, usePrivy } from '@varity-labs/ui-kit';

// Wrap app root
<PrivyStack appId={process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_PRIVY_APP_ID}>
  <App />
</PrivyStack>

// Auth hook
const { user, authenticated, logout, ready } = usePrivy();
const email = user?.email?.address;
```

Components: PrivyStack, PrivyLoginButton, PrivyProtectedRoute, PrivyUserProfile, PrivyReadyGate, VarityDashboardProvider, ThemeProvider, ToastProvider, DashboardLayout, DashboardHeader, DashboardSidebar, DashboardFooter, Button, Input, Textarea, Select, Card, Dialog, ConfirmDialog, Toast, DataTable, Toggle, Checkbox, RadioGroup, Avatar, ProgressBar, Skeleton, Breadcrumb, DropdownMenu, CommandPalette, Sidebar, KPICard, AnalyticsCard, ChartContainer, MetricDisplay, Sparkline, EnhancedKPICard, StatusBadge, IntegrationStatus, EmptyState, LoadingSkeleton, PaymentWidget, PaymentGate

### @varity-labs/types (v2.0.0-beta.8)
TypeScript type definitions for all Varity interfaces: DatabaseConfig, QueryOptions, Document, CollectionResponse, CredentialConfig.

## CLI: varitykit (v1.2.9)

Install: `pip install varitykit`

Commands:
- `varitykit doctor` â€” Check environment and dependencies
- `varitykit init [name]` â€” Scaffold new project from template
- `varitykit app deploy` â€” Deploy to production (auto-detects framework)
- `varitykit app deploy --submit-to-store` â€” Deploy and submit to App Store
- `varitykit app deploy --hosting dynamic` â€” Deploy full-stack app with compute
- `varitykit app list` â€” List your deployments
- `varitykit app info <id>` â€” Get deployment details
- `varitykit app status` â€” View overall deployment status
- `varitykit app rollback <id>` â€” Revert to a previous deployment
- `varitykit platforms` â€” Show available deployment platforms

Deploy options: `--hosting static|dynamic`, `--path .`, `--submit-to-store`, `--tier free|starter|growth|enterprise|scale`, `--name <app-name>`, `--mode auto|guided|expert`

## Templates

### SaaS Starter (default)
Production-ready Next.js 14 template with:
- Authentication (email, Google, GitHub, Twitter, Discord)
- Dashboard with sidebar navigation
- Settings page (profile, preferences, security, billing)
- Landing page with animations
- Command palette (Cmd+K)
- 20+ UI components
- Database integration

Create: `npx create-varity-app my-app`

## Development Pattern

Type -> Collection -> Hook -> Page:

1. Define TypeScript interface in `src/types/index.ts`
2. Create collection accessor in `src/lib/database.ts`
3. Build React hook with optimistic updates in `src/lib/hooks.ts`
4. Use hook in page component

## Auth Pattern

```typescript
// Layout: wrap with PrivyStack
<PrivyStack appId={process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_PRIVY_APP_ID}>
  {children}
</PrivyStack>

// Protected routes
<PrivyProtectedRoute>
  <DashboardContent />
</PrivyProtectedRoute>

// Access user
const { user, authenticated, logout } = usePrivy();
```

## Deploy

```bash
npm run build
varitykit app deploy
# App is live at https://varity.app/my-app/
```

Static hosting and dynamic hosting auto-detected.
Credentials injected automatically at deploy time.
Supported frameworks: Next.js, React (Vite), React (CRA), Vue, custom.

## Environment Variables

- `NEXT_PUBLIC_PRIVY_APP_ID` â€” Auth provider ID (optional for dev, shared creds used)
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_VARITY_APP_ID` â€” Your app ID (set after first deploy)
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_VARITY_APP_TOKEN` â€” Database token (for local dev)
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_VARITY_DB_PROXY_URL` â€” Database proxy URL (for local dev)

Also supports `VITE_` and `REACT_APP_` prefixes for Vite and CRA projects.

## Monetization

Submit to Varity App Store: `varitykit app deploy --submit-to-store`
Revenue split: 90% developer / 10% Varity
Users pay with credit cards (no crypto required)

## Key Rules for AI Assistants

- NEVER use blockchain/crypto terminology in user-facing code
- Use "deploy" not "upload to IPFS"
- Use "sign in" not "connect wallet"
- Use "usage fees are covered" not "gasless transactions"
- Use TypeScript interfaces for all data models
- Use optimistic UI updates (update state first, then API, rollback on error)
- Use `'use client'` directive for hooks and interactive components
- `.get()` returns all docs â€” filter on client side
- No `.where()` method â€” all filtering is client-side
- DashboardLayout has NO mobile support â€” implement mobile nav manually
- Database credentials are auto-injected at deploy time
- Static export: use `output: 'export'` in next.config.js
- No dynamic routes ([id]) with static export â€” use client-side state
- Dialog prop is `open` (not `isOpen`)
- Documents returned from `.add()`, `.get()`, `.update()` are flat â€” access `doc.name` not `doc.data.name`

Full docs: https://docs.varity.so

- SDK Reference: https://docs.varity.so/packages/sdk/overview/

- CLI Reference: https://docs.varity.so/cli/overview/

- Templates: https://docs.varity.so/templates/saas-starter/

- Authentication: https://docs.varity.so/build/auth/quickstart/

- MCP Server: https://docs.varity.so/ai-tools/mcp-server-spec/
- Troubleshooting: https://docs.varity.so/resources/troubleshooting/
- FAQ: https://docs.varity.so/resources/faq/

## MCP Server

Install: `npx -y @varity-labs/mcp`
15 tools: varity_search_docs, varity_cost_calculator, varity_doctor, varity_login, varity_init, varity_install_deps, varity_build, varity_add_collection, varity_create_repo, varity_deploy, varity_deploy_status, varity_deploy_logs, varity_submit_to_store, varity_migrate, varity_open_browser

Setup for Cursor (.cursor/mcp.json):
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "varity": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@varity-labs/mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

Setup for Claude Code:
```bash
claude mcp add varity -- npx -y @varity-labs/mcp
```

- Discord: https://discord.gg/7vWsdwa2Bg
- GitHub: https://github.com/varity-labs/varity-sdk
